personal.name: Sai Krishna Bykani
personal.title: Software Development Engineer in Test (SDET)
personal.summary: I work as a Software Development Engineer in Test (SDET), building enterprise-grade automation frameworks for payment systems. My work spans UI automation, API automation, backend validation, performance engineering, CI/CD integration, and release quality assurance for mission-critical financial applications.

experience.domain: Payment Gateway, Merchant Acquiring, Acquiring Switch, Clearing & Settlement, Card Payments, UPI, API Integrations, Merchant Onboarding

UI Automation: Designed and maintained scalable Selenium WebDriver automation frameworks using Java, TestNG, and Cucumber BDD.
Automated merchant onboarding, checkout, payment processing, and administrative workflows.
Developed reusable Page Object Model components to improve framework maintainability.
Reduced regression execution time through automated end-to-end test suites.
API Automation: Designed comprehensive REST Assured automation suites for payment APIs.
Backend Validation: Verified transaction data using MySQL and MongoDB.
Performed reconciliation checks between UI, APIs, and databases.
Executed JDBC validations for financial transactions.
Performance Engineering: Executed load, stress, spike, and endurance testing.
Analyzed TPS, latency, throughput, and response times.
Monitored system health using Grafana and Dynatrace.
Validated application stability under production-scale workloads.
CI/CD & Framework: Integrated automation suites with Jenkins pipelines.
Supported release validation and regression execution.
Collaborated with developers during sprint releases.
Improved automation reusability using modular framework design.

achievements: Built reusable UI and API automation frameworks.
Improved software quality through continuous automation.

projects.title: PSP – Enterprise Payment Services Platform
projects.summary: Core payment gateway handling merchant onboarding, acquiring switch, clearing, settlement, and reconciliation.

caseStudy.overview: Worked as a Software Development Engineer in Test (SDET) on a large-scale enterprise Payment Services Platform responsible for managing the complete lifecycle of digital payment transactions.
caseStudy.businessDomain: Payment Gateway, Merchant Acquiring, Clearing & Settlement, Reconciliations
caseStudy.businessProblem: Financial payment platforms must process millions of secure transactions with near-zero failure rates. Any issue in authorization, routing, clearing, or settlement can directly impact merchants and banks.
caseStudy.businessContext: The system processes high-volume Card and UPI transactions, requiring an automated validation approach that guarantees zero defects in production.
responsibilities: Designed the end-to-end regression automation framework from scratch.
Automated critical merchant checkout and onboarding workflows.
Validated ISO-8583 transaction messaging across the acquiring switch.
Built data-driven API suites for complex payload validations.
caseStudy.testingStrategy: A decoupled, risk-based approach prioritizing API contracts first, followed by UI workflows, and finally performance testing.
caseStudy.framework: Data-Driven Page Object Model using Java, Cucumber, and TestNG.
caseStudy.uiAutomation: Implemented highly scalable Selenium scripts handling dynamic iframes (payment pages) and multi-tab workflows for merchant onboarding.
caseStudy.apiAutomation: Developed REST Assured suites utilizing POJOs and Jackson for robust payload serialization and deserialization.
caseStudy.dbValidation: Wrote complex JDBC wrappers to cross-verify MongoDB transaction logs against MySQL settlement ledgers.
caseStudy.performanceTesting: Injected loads of 5,000+ TPS using distributed Apache JMeter to locate database locking and memory leak bottlenecks.
caseStudy.cicd: Automated regression execution via Jenkins nightly builds with custom ExtentReports.
challenges: Dynamic Webhooks: Overcame asynchronous webhook validation by polling database states.
Stale Elements in React UI: Implemented custom fluent waits resolving 99% of synchronization flakiness.
caseStudy.impact: Drastically reduced post-release hotfixes and automated 95% of the manual regression cycle.
achievements: Identified memory leaks at 3x peak load.
Automated 2,000+ test scenarios across UI and API.
testingMetrics: Execution time reduced from 2 days to 3 hours
caseStudy.lessonsLearned: API-first testing significantly reduces flakiness compared to UI-heavy validation in Fintech applications.

projects.title: RWA – Residential Welfare Association Platform
projects.summary: A comprehensive community management platform covering resident onboarding, visitor approvals, and maintenance payments.

caseStudy.overview: An end-to-end residential management platform automating everything from resident approvals to facility bookings.
caseStudy.businessProblem: Managing large residential complexes manually leads to visitor delays, payment tracking issues, and miscommunication.
caseStudy.businessContext: The platform required rigorous testing to ensure security and smooth operations for thousands of residents concurrently.
responsibilities: Automated the entire resident onboarding flow.
Validated the visitor approval notification triggers.
caseStudy.testingStrategy: Component-based automation focusing heavily on role-based access control (RBAC).
caseStudy.framework: Hybrid Framework with Java, Selenium, and TestNG.
caseStudy.uiAutomation: Automated resident dashboards and admin portals.
caseStudy.dbValidation: Ensured transactional integrity for maintenance payments.
caseStudy.performanceTesting: Executed API load testing for peak visitor hours.
caseStudy.cicd: Configured automated sanity runs on every pull request.
challenges: RBAC Testing: Handling dynamic permissions across 5+ distinct user roles.
caseStudy.impact: Streamlined deployments and ensured zero regressions in the payment module.
achievements: Prevented critical security flaws in visitor approvals.
caseStudy.lessonsLearned: Data-driven testing is crucial for applications with highly dynamic permission structures.

projects.title: PPMS – NGO Project Management System
projects.summary: Non-profit management system streamlining donor management, beneficiary tracking, campaigns, and donations.

caseStudy.overview: A dedicated management platform for NGOs automating administrative workflows and donation tracking.
caseStudy.businessProblem: NGOs require transparent tracking of donations, efficient volunteer allocation, and accurate reporting to maintain donor trust.
caseStudy.businessContext: Financial transparency is paramount, making data validation and reporting accuracy the highest testing priorities.
responsibilities: End-to-end automation of the donation pipeline.
Cross-browser compatibility testing for the donor portal.
Complex database queries to validate report generation.
caseStudy.testingStrategy: Data-centric approach heavily utilizing BDD to ensure business requirements directly translated into automated checks.
caseStudy.framework: BDD Framework with Cucumber, Java, and Selenium.
caseStudy.uiAutomation: Automated donor journeys across Chrome, Firefox, and Safari.
caseStudy.dbValidation: Wrote complex SQL joins to verify donation ledgers matched campaign totals.
caseStudy.performanceTesting: Simulated donation spikes during marketing campaigns.
caseStudy.cicd: Integrated tests into Jenkins nightly pipelines.
challenges: Complex Reporting: Validating multi-layered PDF and Excel reports.
caseStudy.impact: Ensured absolute financial transparency and reduced manual reporting verification efforts significantly.
achievements: Built a highly readable BDD suite for non-technical stakeholders.
caseStudy.lessonsLearned: Cucumber BDD excels when business stakeholders need high visibility into the testing process.

projects.title: VCIP – Video Customer Identification Platform
projects.summary: Digital banking platform enabling highly secure Video KYC, customer onboarding, and identity verification.

caseStudy.overview: A RegTech solution for remote customer onboarding relying heavily on real-time video streaming and external identity APIs.
caseStudy.businessProblem: Digital banking requires highly secure, compliant, and fraud-resistant identity verification mechanisms before onboarding.
caseStudy.businessContext: The system must handle heavy video streams concurrently while connecting to government APIs (Aadhaar/PAN) without timeouts.
responsibilities: Performance engineering of the video streaming APIs.
Automated verification of PAN/Aadhaar integration endpoints.
caseStudy.testingStrategy: Performance-first testing combined with strict API security validations to ensure compliance.
caseStudy.framework: REST Assured for API, JMeter for Performance.
caseStudy.apiAutomation: Deep validation of identity verification APIs, focusing on timeout handling.
caseStudy.dbValidation: Verified audit logs for compliance tracking.
caseStudy.performanceTesting: Pushed video streaming endpoints to failure points to determine system breaking thresholds.
challenges: Mocking Govt APIs: Created robust stub services to simulate Aadhaar/PAN downtime and rate limits.
caseStudy.impact: Ensured 100% compliance with KYC regulations and enabled seamless onboarding under heavy load.
achievements: Tuned system to handle 10,000 concurrent KYC sessions.
Identified and resolved critical memory leaks in the streaming engine.
caseStudy.lessonsLearned: Resiliency testing (mocking external API failures) is critical for systems relying on third-party integrations.
